pattern: The next permutation is found by locating the rightmost ascending step, swapping that pivot with the smallest larger value to its right, then reversing the suffix into ascending order.
hints: Permutations in increasing order have a structure: a long descending tail means you are at a local maximum and must change something earlier.
Find the rightmost position where a value is smaller than its neighbor to the right; everything after it is non-increasing.

Let i be that pivot; swap nums[i] with the smallest element to its right that still exceeds it, then reverse nums[i+1:] so the suffix becomes its smallest arrangement. If no pivot exists, reverse the whole array.

edge_inputs.description: pivot deep inside with a descending tail
needs reverse of a multi-element suffix

stress.description: small arrays with possible repeats so the pivot/suffix logic is exercised
stress.note: small value range introduces duplicates that stress the strictly-greater swap
